# Source Card: Guidelines for the diagnosis and management of adult aplastic anaemia (BSH 2024)

## Summary
This British Society for Haematology 2024 national guideline defines the peripheral-count diagnostic thresholds for aplastic anaemia (AA) and the Camitta severity grading (severe / very severe) that drive treatment pathways. For a pediatric CDS module it supplies fixed, age-independent disease-severity cutoffs (neutrophils, platelets, reticulocytes, marrow cellularity) plus an important assay-method caveat: the reticulocyte criterion is defined for AUTOMATED analyzers (<60 ×10⁹/L). It is an ADULT-scoped guideline and defines no pediatric age-specific reference intervals; its cutoffs are severity thresholds, not reference intervals, and must not be conflated with age-adjusted CBC norms.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) AA diagnostic thresholds: ≥2 of Hb <100 g/L, platelets <50 ×10⁹/L, neutrophils <1.5 ×10⁹/L — locator: Diagnosis intro — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:561346298877261606eaed5e5d3d44fbcc47e5479d8f7ddf4640bb92836bf6c6]"
- (ev_002) Severe AA (Camitta): cellularity <25% (or 25–50% with <30% residual) + ≥2 of neutrophils <0.5, platelets <20, reticulocytes <60 — locator: severity definition — quote as recorded.
- (ev_003) Very severe AA: as SAA but neutrophils <0.2 ×10⁹/L — locator: VSAA line.
- (ev_004) Reticulocyte criterion modified to absolute <60 ×10⁹/L for automated analyzers (which over-estimate vs manual) — locator: reticulocyte investigation row.
- (ev_005) Marrow cellularity: overall average <30% (excluding lymphocytes/plasma cells) — locator: trephine biopsy paragraph.
- (ev_006) Biphasic incidence: first peak 10–25 years, 2–3 per million/year (Europe) — locator: epidemiology intro.
- (ev_007) Strong recommendation for MDT discussion / expert-centre involvement (referral trigger) — locator: introduction/abstract.

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: ADULT acquired aplastic anaemia. The Camitta cutoffs are absolute-count disease-severity thresholds applied irrespective of age; the same Camitta framework is used in pediatric AA but this source does not restate pediatric-specific values, so pediatric applicability is not established here.
- Assay dependence: reticulocyte severity cutoff (<60 ×10⁹/L) is explicitly for AUTOMATED reticulocyte counting; the original manual-percentage Camitta value differs. Marrow cellularity requires a ≥2 cm trephine biopsy with morphological assessment excluding lymphocytes/plasma cells.
- Lifecycle: BSH 2024 guideline (Br J Haematol 204(3):784–804), effective 2024-01; supersedes the prior BSH 2016 AA guideline (Killick et al., doi:10.1111/bjh.13853). The reticulocyte sub-criterion supersedes the original manual Camitta percentage.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes. ev_007 is a referral/escalation trigger: rarity and complexity mandate MDT discussion and expert-centre involvement. Severe/very-severe grading (ev_002/ev_003) functions as an urgency/scope-exit boundary (VSAA neutrophils <0.2 ×10⁹/L marks the highest-risk band). For a pediatric CDS, a suspected AA pattern (pancytopenia + hypocellular marrow) should route to specialist haematology, not be resolved in-module.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Adult scope: this source does NOT define pediatric age-specific CBC reference intervals; its numbers are severity cutoffs, not reference intervals — do not average or merge them with age-adjusted pediatric norms.
- Quotes are from the open-access accepted manuscript (SGUL repository eprint 116261); the version-of-record (Wiley, paywalled) may differ in copy-edit/typography. Superscript exponents (×10⁹/l) were flattened to "109/l" in the source's extracted text and are transcribed here as ×10⁹/l.
- The guideline also states prophylactic platelet-transfusion thresholds (e.g. <10 ×10⁹/L on active treatment) — deliberately NOT asserted here because transfusion directives are out of scope for the CDS proposal.
- No direct conflict recorded with other carded sources in this run; if a pediatric AA source (e.g. an EBMT/SAAWP pediatric guideline) restates different Camitta reticulocyte handling, preserve it as a conflict rather than collapsing.
